title: Node.js module 'url' is disallowed in Code node
symptom: The Code node fails to execute, throwing an error that the module 'node:url' is disallowed. This occurs when moving workflows to a self-hosted environment.
applies_when: Running n8n in a Docker container
not_this_if: The error does not occur in the original hosting environment
root_cause: n8n restricts access to certain Node.js built-in modules for security reasons. If the environment variables are not correctly set in the Docker container, it may block access to these modules, leading to the error.
fix_steps: Verify that the environment variable NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN is correctly set to include 'url'.
Ensure that NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_EXTERNAL is set to '*' if using external packages.
Restart the Docker container to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Check and update the Docker environment variables for n8n, then restart the container.
